title: cyrtanthus obliquus
intro: Cyrtanthus obliquus, a member of the family Amaryllidaceae, is a flowering plant also known as the Yellow or Orange Sabri or sometimes simply as the Yellow Cyrtanthus, native to South Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Cyrtanthus obliquus include its ability to aid in the treatment of various ailments, such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, with some herbal preparations also involving the use of its leaves and flowers in infusions or decoctions.
